note:
浮躁的新闻初创公司Axios押注，公司将为记者的写作付出高昂的代价。
  该公司下个月将启动一个交流平台AxiosHQ，该平台将使企业能够以Axios的事实，要点形式（包括通过内部新闻通讯）更新其员工。
  Axios称，该软件工具每年的成本至少为10,000美元，具体取决于客户的规模，这是数字新闻初创公司推出的首款付费订阅产品。 Axios成立于2017年，主要报道美国政治，媒体和技术，并以其简短而独特的故事而闻名。
  Axios联合创始人兼总裁Roy Schwartz说，该平台的订户可以选择添加一项服务，使他们可以利用Axios新闻部门以外的编辑团队来撰写提示。施瓦茨说，该团队的一位成员过去曾帮助编辑特朗普总统的总统每日简报。 AxiosHQ还具有一个工具，可根据Axios员工的编辑数据库来建议语法和用法更改。
    该产品是Axios首席执行官Jim VandeHei建立订阅业务的愿望的一部分，他在公司成立时就表示了这一愿望。去年获利的Axios没有付费专区，目前大部分收入来自广告。
  VandeHei先生在一封电子邮件采访中说，他一直希望Axios的沟通方式成为公司订购解决方案的一部分，他将这种沟通方式描述为“成倍提高效率”。一位知情人士说，Axios正在考虑使用该工具来帮助公司进行外部沟通，例如投资者关系。
  Schwartz先生说，他预计该产品今年将产生超过200万美元的收入，已经预订了100万美元。他说，达美航空DAL 2.52％和AT＆amp; T T 2.38％是已经签约的20个客户之一，但拒绝透露他们要付多少钱。 AT＆amp; T确认正在使用该服务。达美航空没有回应置评请求。 
知情人士说，未来版本的AxiosHQ可能包括Axios上免费提供的独家故事。
  AxiosHQ将与主要技术公司制造的应用程序竞争，但是Schwartz先生说，他希望该工具的书写辅助功能将有助于使其在竞争中脱颖而出。他说，他不希望该产品引起利益冲突，因为AxiosHQ团队与该公司的记者和编辑是分开的。
  一位知情人士表示，Axios在2020年的收入超过6000万美元，比前一年增长约40％。该公司最近一直在扩大本地新闻业务，在明尼阿波利斯发布新闻通讯。丹佛佛罗里达州坦帕;和得梅因，爱荷华州。它还同意收购当地媒体创业公司夏洛特议程。
  Axios已从Lerer Hippeau，Comcast NBCUniversal和Greycroft Partners等公司筹集了5700万美元。据《华尔街日报》报道，该公司在2019年12月的最后一轮融资中获得2亿美元的融资前估值。
  出现在2021年1月15日的印刷版上，它是“新的Axios平台为企业进行浮躁写作提供提示”。
note_en:
Punchy news startup Axios is betting that companies will pay big bucks to write like its reporters.
  The company next month will launch AxiosHQ, a communications platform that will enable businesses to update their employees—including through internal newsletters—in Axios’s just-the-facts, bullet-point style.
  The software tool, which Axios said would cost at least $10,000 a year depending on a customer’s size, is the first paid-subscription product launched by the digital-news startup. Founded in 2017, Axios mostly covers U.S. politics, media and technology, and is known for its short, exclusive stories.
  Subscribers to the platform have the option to add a service that allows them to tap a team of editors—separate from Axios’s news division—for writing tips, Axios co-founder and President Roy Schwartz said. One member of that team used to help edit the presidential daily briefing for President Trump, Mr. Schwartz said. AxiosHQ also features a tool that recommends grammar and usage changes based on a database of edits from Axios staff.
    The product is part of Axios Chief Executive Jim VandeHei’s desire to build a subscription business, which he stated when the company was founded. Axios, which was profitable last year, doesn’t have a paywall and currently generates most of its revenue through advertising.
  In an email interview, Mr. VandeHei said he always wanted Axios’s communication style, which he described as “exponentially more efficient,” to be part of the company’s subscription solutions. Axios is pondering applying the tool to help companies with external communications such as investor relations, a person familiar with the matter said.
  Mr. Schwartz said he expects the product to generate more than $2 million in revenue this year, with $1 million already booked. He said  Delta Air Lines    DAL  2.52%  and  AT&T    T  2.38%  are among the 20 clients that have already signed on, but declined to disclose how much they are paying. AT&T confirmed it is using the service. Delta didn’t respond to a request for comment.
  Future versions of AxiosHQ might include exclusive stories not available for free on Axios, according to people familiar with the matter.
  AxiosHQ will compete with applications made by major technology companies, but Mr. Schwartz said he expects the tool’s writing-assistance features will help it stand out from the competition. He said he doesn’t expect the product to result in conflicts of interest, because the AxiosHQ team is separate from the company’s reporters and editors.
  Axios  took in more than $60 million in revenue in 2020, up about 40% from the year before, according to a person familiar with the matter. The company has recently been expanding its local-news presence, launching newsletters in Minneapolis; Denver; Tampa, Fla.; and Des Moines, Iowa. It also agreed to acquire local media startup Charlotte Agenda.
  Axios has raised $57 million from firms including Lerer Hippeau,  Comcast   NBCUniversal and Greycroft Partners. It was given a pre-money valuation of $200 million in  its last funding round in December 2019, The Wall Street Journal reported.
  Appeared in the January 15, 2021, print edition as 'New Axios Platform Offers Tips to Firms On Punchy Writing.'
title: Axios表示将启动AxiosHQ，该公司可以在下个月开始编写类似Axios新闻稿的内部备忘录，起价为每年1万美元
title_en: Axios says it will launch AxiosHQ, which lets companies write internal memos styled like Axios newsletters, next month, starting at $10K a year

note:
OneWeb在12月发射的36颗新卫星使在轨总数达到110颗。这意味着第一代还剩下500多个发射器。
 该公司正在继续削减其野心。本周早些时候，OneWeb宣布已“精简其星座”，因此减少了向美国监管机构提出的许可要求。最初，OneWeb已向FCC申请了47,884颗卫星的市场准入；现在这一数字已降至6,372。
  该公司表示，软银集团公司和休斯网络系统公司正在提供融资。这一消息是在OneWeb发射了36颗卫星后一个月发布的，这是第三次发射以使更多机队进入轨道。当时，其执行主席苏尼尔·巴蒂·米塔尔（Sunil Bharti Mittal）表示，它有望筹集4亿美元，因此，这一数字增长了三倍多。这似乎证实了这一点。
 “ OneWeb的使命是使每个地方的每个人都联系在一起。自从11月从第11章起步以来，我们在重新启动业务方面取得了快速进展。” OneWeb首席执行官Neil Masterson表示。 “我们欢迎软银和休斯的投资，以进一步证明在实现我们的目标方面取得的进展。”
 该公司的一位发言人向我证实，该公司未披露其估值。加上这一轮融资，该公司迄今为止似乎已经筹集了大约45亿美元，尽管破产意味着对该公司进行了大笔的资本重组和重估，而且这个数字包括重组前的资金。
 SoftBank和Hughes都是OneWeb的既往支持者和合作伙伴，因此，这是一项保险政策，以确保其先前的投资不会完全浪费掉。 （至少其中一些已经被减记：多年前，软银公布了令人eye目结舌的240亿美元亏损，部分原因是OneWeb的押注。）
 同时，休斯通过其母公司EchoStar进行投资，并于2017年与该公司签署了一项协议，以建造可与OneWeb卫星配合使用的地面基础设施。卫星技术世界中的交易，建造和部署历经数年之久，并且常常会面临延误，因此，如果能看到三年的交易成果，甚至三年甚至更长，就不足为奇了。 
OneWeb承认投资者之间的长期联系，并确认休斯仍在建立地面网络。
 “我们很高兴欢迎软银和休斯的投资。双方都对我们的业务非常熟悉，分享我们对未来的看法，他们的承诺使我们能够利用OneWeb未来的重大增长机会。”米塔尔在一份声明中说。 “我们从他们的经验和能力中受益，我们为全球提供了独特的LEO网络。”
 最初，休斯计划在2019年开始运行第一批服务，尽管那时候OneWeb及其LEO（低地球轨道）卫星舰队仍然是一个非常闪亮的主意，并得到了17亿美元的风险投资的支持。
 该公司的最初想法总是很棒，但（绝非双关语）也有些月球：LEO卫星已经被证明是对地面网络的强大而有用的补充，可以为无法到达的偏远地区提供宽带连接。其他方法。 OneWeb的想法是使该服务有用，并被更多的地面用户使用，并且每个人的承诺是400Mbps。
 虽然过渡时期的宽带使用肯定会激增，但OneWeb可能没有想到的是，那些构建非卫星系统以提供连接性的人也将在网络方面取得进步。也不会花多少时间或所需的资金来使它的机队按其有希望的时间表启动。
 如今，OneWeb表示，不断增长的5G，物联网和连接需求在总体上仍显示出其方法的强大用例-它表示“包括全球网关站网络和针对不同客户市场的一系列用户终端，提供负担得起的，快速的，高带宽和低延迟的通信服务。”
 北爱尔兰国务卿国务卿。老公夸西·克瓦滕（Kwasi Kwarteng）在一份声明中说：“我们对OneWeb的投资是我们对英国航天领域的持续承诺的一部分，使英国处于最新技术进步的最前沿。今天的投资使该公司更进一步地实现了为个人，企业和政府提供全球宽带连接的使命，同时有可能在英国释放新的研究，开发和制造机会。” 
“ OneWeb不断增加容量并加速实现商业化，我们很高兴能为它提供支持，”董事长兼公司总裁，公司董事代表孙正义说。软银首席执行官在一份声明中。 “我们很高兴继续与Bharti，英国政府和休斯（Hughes）建立合作伙伴关系，以帮助OneWeb履行其使命，改变世界各地的互联网访问。”
 休斯（Hughes）总裁普拉德曼·考尔（Pradman Kaul）补充说：“ OneWeb继续激励该行业，并吸引业务中的最佳参与者汇聚一堂，共同实现其LEO星座。休斯和软银今天进行的投资将帮助实现OneWeb在连接企业，政府和移动客户方面的全部潜力，尤其是通过多传输服务来补充我们自己的对地静止产品，以满足并加速全球对宽带的需求。”
 更新以澄清这14亿美元包括今天宣布的4亿美元，以及先前的资金。
note_en:
The 36 new satellites OneWeb launched in December brought the total number in orbit to 110 satellites. This means there are still more than 500 left to launch in the first generation.
 The company is continuing to whittle down its ambitions. Earlier this week, OneWeb  announced that it had “streamlined its constellation” and as a result was reducing the request it was making to the U.S. regulators for licenses. Originally OneWeb had applied to the FCC for market access for 47,884 satellites; now the figure is down to 6,372.
  SoftBank Group Corp. and Hughes Network Systems are providing the financing, the company said. The news comes about a month after OneWeb launched 36 satellites, its third launch to put more of its fleet into orbit. At the time, its executive chairman  Sunil Bharti Mittal said that it was on track to raise $400 million  — so this represents a more-than threefold increase on that amount. This appears to confirm that.
 “OneWeb’s mission is to connect everyone, everywhere. We have made rapid progress to re-start the business since emerging from Chapter 11 in November,” said Neil Masterson, CEO of OneWeb, in a statement. “We welcome the investments by SoftBank and Hughes as further proof of progress towards delivering our goal.”
 A spokesperson for the company has confirmed to me that the company is not disclosing its valuation. Adding in this round, it looks like the company has raised around $4.5 billion to date, although the bankruptcy meant a significant recapitalization and revaluation of the business and that figure includes funding from before it was restructured.
 SoftBank and Hughes are both past backers and partners in OneWeb, so this is something of an insurance policy to make sure that its previous investment doesn’t go completely to waste. (At least some of it has already been written down: SoftBank years ago  posted an eye-watering loss of $24 billion due in part to that OneWeb bet.)
 Hughes, meanwhile, invests via its parent company EchoStar and  inked a deal with the company way back in 2017 to build the terrestrial infrastructure that would work with OneWeb’s satellites. Deals, building and rollouts in the world of satellite technology play out over a number of years, and often face delays, so being three years out — or even more — on seeing any fruits from that deal is not hugely surprising.
 OneWeb acknowledged the long-time connection between the investors and confirmed that the ground network is still being built by Hughes.
 “We are delighted to welcome the investment from SoftBank and Hughes. Both are deeply familiar with our business, share our vision for the future, and their commitment allows us to capitalise on the significant growth opportunity ahead for OneWeb,” said Mittal in a statement. “We gain from their experience and capabilities, as we deliver a unique LEO network for the world.”
 Originally, Hughes had planned for the first services to start running in 2019 — although that was when OneWeb and its fleet of LEO (low-earth orbit) satellites was still a very shiny idea, backed by $1.7 billion in venture funding.
 The company’s original idea was always great but (no pun intended) also something of a moonshot: LEO satellites have already been proven to be a strong and useful complement to terrestrial networks for providing broadband connectivity to more remote areas that couldn’t be reached in other ways. The idea with OneWeb was to make that service something useful and used by a much bigger group of on-the-ground users, with  the promise being 400Mbps for everyone.
 While broadband usage has certainly exploded in the interim, what OneWeb perhaps didn’t bank on was that those building non-satellite systems for providing connectivity would also be progressing in their network advances; nor how long it might take, or the financing needed, to get its fleet off the ground on the timelines it was promising.
 These days, OneWeb says that growing ubiquity of 5G, Internet of Things and connectivity needs overall still present a strong use case for its approach — which it says “ includes a network of global gateway stations and a range of user terminals for different customer markets capable of delivering affordable, fast, high-bandwidth and low-latency communications services.”
 Secretary of State, BEIS, The Rt. Hon. Kwasi Kwarteng, said in a statement: “Our investment in OneWeb is part of our continued commitment to the UK’s space sector, putting Britain at the forefront of the latest technological advances. Today’s investment brings the company one step closer to delivering its mission to provide global broadband connectivity for people, businesses and governments, while potentially unlocking new research, development and manufacturing opportunities in the UK.”
  “We are excited to support OneWeb as it increases capacity and accelerates towards commercialisation,” said Masayoshi Son, Representative Director, Corporate Officer, Chairman & CEO of SoftBank, in a statement. “We are thrilled to continue our partnership with Bharti, the UK Government and Hughes to help OneWeb deliver on its mission to transform internet access around the world.”
 Pradman Kaul, President of Hughes, added: “OneWeb continues to inspire the industry and attract the best players in the business to come together to bring its LEO constellation to fruition. The investments made today by Hughes and SoftBank will help realise the full potential of OneWeb in connecting enterprise, government and mobility customers, especially with multi-transport services that complement our own geostationary offerings in meeting and accelerating demand for broadband around the world.”
 Updated to clarify that the $1.4 billion includes $400 million announced today, plus previous funding.
title: OneWeb迄今已发射了648个卫星的计划星座中的110个以提供互联网访问，并从SoftBank和Hughes筹集了1.4亿美元资金和4亿美元资金
title_en: OneWeb, which to date launched 110 out of a planned constellation of 648 satellites to provide internet access, raises $1.4B with $400M from SoftBank and Hughes

note:
根据周四提交的一份投诉，亚马逊被指控通过与美国排名前五位的出版商达成反竞争协议来固定在电子商务网站上出售的电子书的价格。
 向纽约南区美国地方法院提起的集体诉讼诉讼称，亚马逊与出版商于2015年签订了定价协议，允许出版商将其电子书价格提高多达30％，同时保护亚马逊免受其他电子书零售商的价格竞争。
 该诉讼还指控亚马逊通过与由Hachette，HarperCollins，Macmillan，Penguin-Random House和Simon＆amp; amp; Co. Ltd.组成的被称为“五巨头”的出版商达成的协议违反了反托拉斯法和保护消费者的法律。舒斯特。
  该公司由Hagens Berman提起诉讼，此前该公司于2011年针对苹果公司和所谓的“五巨头”提起了类似的集体诉讼案。该案以苹果公司以4亿美元和解，出版商又以数百万美元和解而告终。说过。
 和解协议还阻止了五大巨头干扰零售商。诉状称，通过打折两年，导致2013年至2014年电子书的定价更低且更具竞争力，而据称亚马逊与出版商在2015年达成了定价协议。
 哈根斯·伯曼（Hagens Berman）的执行合伙人史蒂夫·伯曼（Steve Berman）在一份声明中说：“亚马逊的权力再次证明，当涉及违反反托拉斯法时，新经济也将采取同样的手段。”
 亚马逊和麦克米伦公司的发言人拒绝置评。其他出版公司的发言人没有立即对此事发表评论。 
该诉讼是在同一周提起的，康涅狄格州司法部长威廉·唐（D）说，他的办公室针对“与某些出版商的电子书发行协议中的潜在反竞争条款”，对亚马逊进行了“积极且正在进行的反托拉斯调查”。  康涅狄格州是先前因电子书销售竞争对苹果提起诉讼的州之一。 司法部在2012年还起诉苹果，指控苹果与主要出版商串谋电子书的定价。  由州领导的对亚马逊电子书业务的调查是电子商务巨头面临的许多反托拉斯调查之一，包括由州领导和联邦政府的努力。
note_en:
Amazon is accused of fixing the price of e-books sold on the e-commerce site through anticompetitive agreements with the nation’s top five publishers, according to  a complaint filed Thursday.
 The class action lawsuit filed in the U.S. District Court of the Southern District of New York alleges that Amazon and the publishers entered into price fixing agreements in 2015, allowing the publishers to increase their e-book prices by up to 30 percent while protecting Amazon from price competition from other e-book retailers.
 The lawsuit also alleges Amazon violated antitrust and consumer-protection laws through the agreements with the publishers known as the “Big Five,” made up of Hachette, HarperCollins, Macmillan, Penguin-Random House and Simon & Schuster.
  The lawsuit, filed by the firm Hagens Berman, follows a similar class action case the firm filed against Apple and the so-called Big Five in 2011. That case ended with Apple settling for $400 million and the publishers settling for millions more, the firm said.
 The settlement also prevented the Big Five from interfering with retailers' discounts for two years, resulting in lower and more competitive e-book pricing in 2013 to 2014, before the alleged price fixing deal Amazon and the publishers entered into in 2015, according to the complaint.
 “Amazon’s abuse of power proves, yet again, that when it comes to violating antitrust laws, the New Economy is up to the same old tricks,” Steve Berman, managing partner of Hagens Berman, said in a statement.
 Spokespeople for Amazon and Macmillan declined to comment.  Spokespeople for the other publishing companies were not immediately available for comment.
  The lawsuit was filed the same week Connecticut Attorney General William Tong (D)  said his office has an “active and ongoing antitrust investigation” into Amazon over “potentially anticompetitive terms in their e-book distribution agreements with certain publishers.”
 Connecticut was among the states that had previously filed a lawsuit against Apple over competition in e-book sales. The Justice Department also sued Apple in 2012 alleging it conspired with major publishers over the pricing of e-books.
 The state-led investigation into Amazon’s e-book business is one of many antitrust investigations facing the e-commerce giant, including state-led and federal efforts.
title: 哈根斯·伯曼（Hagens Berman）起诉亚马逊，指控其与五家大型发行商合谋确定电子书价格； 哈根斯·伯曼（Hagens Berman）之前曾起诉苹果
title_en: Hagens Berman files suit against Amazon alleging it colluded with five big publishers to fix eBook prices; Hagens Berman previously sued Apple for the same

note:
“我们曾试图让1500万人受苦吗？” MeWe创始人马克·温斯坦（Mark Weinstein）告诉OneZero
  “我是一个讨厌您的叛国者的美国人，”在国会大厦骚乱发生两天后，一个名为Chuck Testa的账户发布在MeWe上，MeWe是极右翼极端分子所青睐的另一种社交网络。 “您以为我们不会反击吗？这里没有适合您的地方。他们继续说道，在聊天室中写了一个由2700多名成员组成的Stop Steal小组。 Testa对非信徒的提议解决方案？ “射击队”。
 在整个MeWe中，诸如Stop The Steal和QAnon之类的运动与右翼民兵组织已扎根，因为Facebook和Twitter之类的主流网站做出了举足轻重的举动（可能为时已晚），以摆脱自己的猖of滥用平台。在过去的两个月中，OneZero观察到数十个帖子支持或鼓励对平台上的选举后事件进行暴力回应。在最近几个月发给Parler，Telegram以及Facebook和Twitter的消息，这些消息已经使一些极右翼的社区平台化。MeWe得到了一些特朗普支持者的积极支持，声称他们是“审查制度”的受害者。
  MeWe的会员人数在最近几个月已增长到大约1500万用户。尽管其竞争对手Parler和Gab因未能减轻仇恨言论而被应用程序商店禁止和启动，但MeWe的网站和移动应用程序仍然没有受到影响。现在，它将继承不满的用户，其中一些人认同武装武装分子和阴谋运动。
 MeWe的创建者说，他从未想过为不受约束的演讲建一个家。相反，MeWe被设想为注重隐私的Facebook替代方案。这就解释了为什么公司董事会目前由众多主流人物组成，其中包括万维网发明家蒂姆·伯纳斯-李（Tim Berners-Lee）； Dwayne“ The Rock” Johnson的制作公司总裁Dany Garcia；以及《灵魂系列鸡肉汤》的作者Jack Canfield和Marci Shimoff。
  在接受MeWe创始人兼首席执行官Mark Weinstein的采访时，OneZero询问为何尽管服务条款禁止“仇恨，威胁，有害”和暴力内容以及“跟踪”成员，但极右翼内容仍然存在于平台上，骚扰，霸凌，恐吓或伤害其他用户。” MeWe说，它保留终止违反这些规则的用户帐户的权利。
 “您曾尝试温和1500万人吗？”温斯坦说。 “它们出现在Twitter和Facebook上-无处不在。为什么选择MeWe？我们比那些人更好。” 
温斯坦拒绝透露其聘用多少员工或承包商来审核MeWe内容，但表示人数不到100人，并补充说MeWe正在努力雇用其他主持人。
  MeWe的起源可以追溯到马克·扎克伯格（Mark Zuckerberg）在其哈佛大学宿舍在线使用TheFacebook的五年之前。 1998年，自由主义者企业家马克·温斯坦（Mark Weinstein）创立了SuperGroups.com，这是一个社交网站，自称为“供您和您的朋友在网上聚会的私人场所”。尽管在2000年代初被出售，但SuperGroups为MeWe奠定了基础，MeWe于2016年由Weinstein公开推出。
 温斯坦将MeWe描述为反对Facebook，因为它决定不出售或共享用户数据，并为用户提供“设计时的隐私”选择。在MeWe顾问委员会任职的Berners-Lee并未回应OneZero的置评请求，他声称该平台将“将互联网的力量还给人民”。甚至主流的隐私专家都拥护公司的使命，并承诺不利用他人的个人信息。
 “为什么要选出MeWe？我们比那些人更好。”
 MeWe认为这是保守派和进步派社区的家园，一个简单的搜索即可发现庆祝参议员伯尼·桑德斯（Bernie Sanders），素食主义者和LGBTQ +问题的社区。 Vice News在12月报道说，MeWe被一些香港人采用，作为对Facebook在监视和隐私问题上的一种抵制。
 但是该平台对隐私的关注也吸引了其他社区，例如以民兵组织“ Oath Keepers USA”命名的“ Antifa Exterminators”和“停止强制接种官方”。
 在OneZero观察到的“乔·拜登不是我的总统”小组中，讨论袭击国会大厦的成员谈论了枪击人。 
“我可以说实话吗……我必须把人的生命置于我的视线范围之内，真是令人恐怖的一天！我不希望这样。”一位名叫贾斯汀·亚历山大（Justin Alexander）的用户在小组的聊天室中写道。
 另一名用户名叫Derek Howell的人回答说：“我也不希望这样。” “但是我对此很满意。时间到了。”
 第三个自称Carrie Brownell的成员写道：“我能够放下我们的家犬……如果我能够触发我所爱的事物，我想对我讨厌的人这样做，或者威胁到我的生命将会是容易得多。”
 与Facebook和Twitter相比，在显着的政策执行事件后偶尔会发布透明度报告，相比之下，MeWe对其调节工作几乎看不见。它要求用户报告似乎违反其准则的帖子，组，页面和成员。从事信任和安全工作的员工可以选择删除个人资料或在其上标记“罢工”；对用户的三击将导致他们被删除。
 当被问及主持人如何审视灰色区域内容时，温斯坦说，您“看到时就讨厌”，并引用了“偏见”和“十字记号”之类的字眼以及诸如“该人应被杀害”之类的评论。
 尽管民盟的准则禁止威胁或煽动暴力的内容，但民兵组织在实践中仍未明确其政策的领域。温斯坦说，美国国会警察已就上周的事件与该公司联系，MeWe正在合作。国会警察没有立即回应OneZero的置评请求。
 《今日美国报》最近追溯了一个阴谋论，认为反政府煽动了国会大厦内的暴力，从诸如4chan和Parler之类的边缘场所到国会议员，该国会议员都基于零证据对这一想法进行了假说。 OneZero在MeWe上发现了类似的内容，“乔·拜登不是我的总统”小组的成员写道：“安提法[在]国会大厦内被拍照”，而“安提法是在特朗普支持者冲进国会大厦时煽动特朗普”。骚乱正在发生。 MeWe发布后删除了本文提到的平台上的所有个人和组。 
去年10月，在OneZero关于民兵组织聚集在MeWe上的报告之后，我们报告了民兵似乎正在招募和组织成员的三个团体和六个页面。 MeWe的发言人说，该公司对所有这些产品进行了调查，但只删除了其中的一个。在星期二对该平台进行的搜索中，“民兵”一词不再有任何结果，尽管民兵组织和OneZero已加入的页面仍在该网站上活跃。温斯坦拒绝评论MeWe最近是否对MeWe上的民兵内容实施了搜索禁令或其他限制，只说如果主持人发现违反该网站规定的材料，则将其删除。
 《纽约时报》周三报道，联邦官员在网上监视极端主义活动，已警告全国主要城市的警察局长在就职日当天提防暴力行为的迹象。
 研究美国极端主义运动的研究员兼分析师J.M. Berger告诉OneZero：“在较小的平台上，内容审核是一个巨大的挑战。” “在此阶段应该明确的是，构建社交媒体应用程序或平台的任何努力都需要对其内容模型进行预算和规划，以将其作为业务模式的重要组成部分。”
 Berger补充说：“也就是说，最大的风险与最大的平台有关。” “去平台化是一种管理极端分子如何招募和煽动行为的工具，但这不是100％的解决方案。否认极端主义者可以接触到数百万观众的平台是一个进步，但他们总会找到其他场所。”
 MeWe的规模相对较小，这说明据称从未想要过的用户可以轻松选择据称用于隐私和消费者控制的平台。此外，随着社区迅速迁移到仍将托管这些平台的平台，在网上主持最右派的海外侨民已经成为一种令人讨厌的情况。
 “尽管不完善，但从长远来看，这是一个贸易协会，在该协会中，像Facebook这样的成熟社交媒体平台将与这些[较小公司]共享内容审核资源，”大西洋居民高级研究员Emerson T. Brooking安理会在网上研究极端主义和虚假信息，告诉OneZero。 “极右翼极端分子从Facebook和Twitter逃走将给这些发展中的平台带来巨大压力。”
 布鲁金补充说：“您可以想到污染等问题。” “尽管大型平台已采用了更严格的内容审核标准（以及用于实施这些标准的工具），但多年来，受益于这一有害用户基础，他们受益匪浅。这意味着他们对下游影响负有责任。” 
当被问及它是否参与跨平台审核和信息共享计划时，Twitter的发言人指示OneZero“访问该行业在内容问题上进行合作并以您所描述的确切方式共享资源的所有领域，这些实体也是向其他较小的公司开放。” Twitter的发言人引用：反抗议者全球互联网论坛；基督城电话； WePROTECT；圣克拉拉原则；国家失踪与死亡中心被剥削的儿童，以及国家支持的信息行动自己的公开档案。
  MeWe咨询委员会的几位成员告诉OneZero，其在用户隐私方面的立场吸引了他们，人们应该在Facebook和Twitter等主流平台之外选择。
 “我认为，像所有平台一样，MeWe像所有平台一样，都需要重新评估其立场，” MeWe顾问，麻省理工学院专门研究社会科学和技术的教授Sherry Turkle在接受采访时对OneZero说道。电子邮件。 “它代表了隐私和言论自由，我支持。这与在站点上计划sedition不同。” （Turkle通知OneZero她已经购买了该公司的股票。）
 “如果我们不帮助像Mewe这样的人想要建立一个*良好*的社交网络，解决那些使他们研究其意图和理想在政策和软件中是如何实现的难题，那么我们将拥有Facebook的双寡头垄断以及MeTwitter顾问兼电子前沿基金会技术项目总监乔恩·卡拉斯（Jon Callas）在一封电子邮件中告诉OneZero。
 更新：MeWe在发布后删除了本文中提到的所有激进右翼个人和团体。
note_en:
‘Have you tried to moderate 15 million people?’ MeWe founder Mark Weinstein told OneZero
  “I’m an American who is sick and tired of you traitors,” an account going by the name Chuck Testa posted on MeWe, an alternative social network popular with far-right extremists, two days after the Capitol riots. “Did you think we weren’t going to fight back… There is no place for you. You must be purged,” they continued, writing in the chatroom for a Stop the Steal group of more than 2,700 members. Testa’s proposed solution to the nonbelievers? A “firing squad.”
 Across MeWe, moveme nts such as Stop the Steal and QAnon, along with right-wing militia groups, have taken root as mainstream sites like Facebook and Twitter make sweeping gestures,  arguably too late, to rid their own platforms of rampant abuse.  OneZero observed dozens of posts endorsing or encouraging a violent response to post-election events on the platform over the past two months. And in messages posted to Parler, Telegram, as well as Facebook and Twitter, which have deplatformed some far-right communities in recent months, MeWe has been actively endorsed by some Trump supporters claiming that they’re victims of “censorship.”
  MeWe’s membership has  grown in recent months to roughly 15 million users. And while its competitors Parler and Gab have been banned from app stores and booted by hosting companies for failing to moderate hate speech, MeWe’s website and mobile app remain untouched. It now stands to inherit disgruntled users, some of them identifying with armed militants and conspiracy movements.
 The creator of MeWe says he never wanted to build a home for unfettered speech. Instead, MeWe was envisioned as a privacy-focused Facebook alternative. That explains why the company’s board is currently made up of a wide swath of mainstream figures, including World Wide Web inventor Tim Berners-Lee; Dany Garcia, president of Dwayne “The Rock” Johnson’s production company; and Jack Canfield and Marci Shimoff, authors of the  Chicken Soup for the Soul series.
  In an interview with MeWe founder and CEO Mark Weinstein,  OneZero asked why radical far-right content persists on the platform in spite of its  terms of service prohibiting “hateful, threatening, harmful,” and violent content, as well as members that “stalk, harass, bully, intimidate, or harm another user.” MeWe says it reserves the right to terminate the accounts of users violating these rules.
 “Have you tried to moderate 15 million people?” Weinstein said. “They’re appearing on Twitter and Facebook — they’re everywhere. Why single out MeWe? We’re better at it than those guys are.”
 Weinstein declined to specify how many employees or contractors it employs to moderate MeWe content, but said the number is under 100 individuals, adding that MeWe is working to hire additional moderators.
  MeWe’s origins date back to five years before Mark Zuckerberg took TheFacebook online in his Harvard University dorm. In 1998, libertarian entrepreneur Mark Weinstein founded SuperGroups.com, a social networking site that  billed itself as “a private place for you and your friends to gather on the Web.” Though it was sold in the early 2000s, SuperGroups laid the foundation for MeWe, which Weinstein publicly launched in 2016.
 Weinstein has described MeWe as  the anti-Facebook for its decision to not sell or share user data, and for offering users “ privacy by design” choices. Berners-Lee, who serves on MeWe’s advisory board and did not respond to  OneZero’s request for comment,  has claimed the platform will return “the power of the internet back to the people.” Even mainstream privacy experts have championed the company’s mission and stated commitments to not exploit people’s personal information.
 “Why single out MeWe? We’re better at it than those guys are.”
 MeWe argues that it is home to conservative and progressive communities alike, and a simple search reveals communities celebrating Senator Bernie Sanders, veganism, and LGBTQ+ issues. In December,   Vice News reported that MeWe was adopted by some Hong Kongers as “a form of resistance” against Facebook over surveillance and privacy concerns.
 But the platform’s focus on privacy has attracted other communities as well, such as “Antifa Exterminators,” a group named for the militia organization “Oath Keepers USA,” and “Stop Mandatory Vaccination OFFICIAL.”
 In a “Joe Biden Is Not My President” group observed by  OneZero, members discussing the attack on the Capitol talked about shooting people.
 “Can I be honest … the day I have to put a human life in my scope is gunna be a scary day ! I’m not looking forward to it,” a user named Justin Alexander wrote in the group’s chatroom.
 “I’m not looking forward to it either,” replied another person with the username Derek Howell. “But I’m at peace with it. And it’s time.”
 A third member calling themselves Carrie Brownell wrote: “I was able to put down our family dog … If I was able to pull the trigger on something I love, I imagine doing it to someone I hate, or is threatening my life would be a lot easier.”
 Compared to Facebook and Twitter, which release occasional transparency reports following notable policy enforcement events, MeWe offers little visibility into its moderation efforts. It requests that users report posts, groups, pages, and members who appear to be violating its guidelines. Employees who work in trust and safety can choose to remove profiles or mark them with “strikes”; three strikes against a user will cause them to be removed.
 When asked how moderators deliberate gray-area content, Weinstein said you “know hate when you see it,” citing things like “prejudice” and “swastikas” and comments such as, “this person should be killed.”
 Militias are one area where MeWe’s policies are unclear in practice, even though its guidelines forbid content that is threatening or incites violence. Weinstein said U.S. Capitol Police had contacted the company in regards to last week’s events, and that MeWe is cooperating. Capitol Police did not immediately respond to  OneZero’s request for comment.
 USA Today recently  traced a conspiracy theory that antifa incited violence at the Capitol from fringe sites like 4chan and Parler to members of Congress who parroted the idea based on zero evidence.  OneZero found similar content on MeWe, with members of the “Joe Biden Is Not My President” group writing that “antifa [was] photographed inside [the] Capitol,” and that “antifa was goading Trump supporters when they stormed the Capitol” as the riots were happening. MeWe  deleted all of the individuals and groups on the platform mentioned in this article after publication.
 In October last year, following  OneZero’s report on militia groups converging on MeWe, we reported three groups and six pages where militias appeared to be recruiting and organizing members. A spokesperson for MeWe said the company investigated all of them, but only removed one. In a search on the platform conducted on Tuesday, the term “militia” no longer served any results, though militia groups and pages  OneZero has already joined are still active on the site. Weinstein declined to comment on whether MeWe had recently implemented a search ban or other restrictions around militia content on MeWe, only saying that if moderators find material breaking the site’s rules, it will be removed.
 Federal officials monitoring extremist activity online have warned police chiefs in major cities across the nation to look out for signs of violence on Inauguration Day, the  New York Times  reported on Wednesday.
 “With smaller platforms, content moderation is a big challenge,” J.M. Berger, a researcher and analyst who studies U.S. extremist movements, told  OneZero, commenting on social media networks in general. “What should be clear at this stage is any effort to build a social media app or platform needs to budget and plan for content moderation as a big part of their business model.”
 “That said, the biggest risks are attached to the biggest platforms,” Berger added. “Deplatforming is a tool for managing how extremists recruit and incite, but it’s not a 100% solution. Denying extremists platforms where they can reach audiences of millions is progress, but they’ll always find other venues.”
 MeWe’s relatively modest size speaks to the ease with which a platform purportedly built for privacy and consumer control can be co-opted by a user base it claims to have never wanted. Furthermore, moderating far-right diaspora online has become a whack-a-mole situation, as communities quickly migrate to whichever platforms will still host them.
 “Although imperfect, the long-term solution to this is a trade association in which well-established social media platforms like Facebook would share content moderation resources with these [smaller companies],” Emerson T. Brooking, a resident senior fellow at the Atlantic Council studying extremism and disinformation online, told  OneZero. “The flight of far-right extremists from Facebook and Twitter is going to put an immense strain on these developing platforms.”
 “You can think of the problem like pollution,” Brooking added. “Even though large platforms have adopted stricter content moderation standards (with the tools to enforce them), they have benefited for years from the presence of this toxic user base. This means that they carry some responsibility for the downstream effects.”
 When asked if it engages in cross-platform moderation and information sharing initiatives, a spokesperson for Twitter directed  OneZero “to all the areas in which the industry collaborates on content issues and shares resources in the exact manner you’re describing — these entities are also open to participation from other, smaller companies.” Twitter’s spokesperson cited: the  Global Internet Forum to Counter Protestors;  Christchurch Call;  WePROTECT;  The Santa Clara Principles; the  National Center for Missing & Exploited Children, and its own  publicly available archive of state-backed information operations.
  Several members of  MeWe’s advisory board told  OneZero that its position on user privacy appealed to them and that people should have a choice outside of mainstream platforms such as Facebook and Twitter.
 “I think that MeWe like all platforms needs to reassess its stance if it becomes a place where illegal activity is planned,” Sherry Turkle, a MeWe advisor and professor at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology specializing in social sciences and technology, told  OneZero in an email. “It has stood for privacy and free speech, which I support. That is not the same as planning sedition on a site.” (Turkle informed  OneZero that she had purchased stock in the company.)
 “If we don’t help people like Mewe who want to make a *good* social network, navigate through hard problems that make them examine how their intentions and ideals are implemented in policies and software, then we will have a literal duopoly of Facebook and Twitter,” Jon Callas, also a MeWe advisor as well as director of technology projects at the Electronic Frontier Foundation, told  OneZero in an email.
 Update:  MeWe   deleted  all of the radical rightwing individuals and groups mentioned in this article after publication.
title: 最右翼人士涌向MeWe，这是一个以隐私为重点的Facebook替代方案，其董事会成员是Tim Berners-Lee，最近几个月的会员人数增长到1500万
title_en: The far-right is flocking to MeWe, a privacy-focused Facebook alternative that has Tim Berners-Lee on its board, with membership growing to 15M in recent months

Spark Capital领导本轮比赛，Jared Leto，Aaron Levie，Jeremy Stoppelman，Max Levchin和Ali Rowghani也参加了比赛。美国运通资深人士Ash Gupta也以顾问身份加入公司-他曾是美国运通的首席风险官。
 该公司表示，它已经吸引了将近30万名申请者。我在9月份报道了X1卡，它吸引了很多读者，所以这个数目并不令我感到惊讶。
  X1卡是具有不同起源故事的不锈钢Visa信用卡。当您申请一张卡时，该公司希望查看您当前和将来的收入，而不是根据您的信用评分来确定您的限额。
 这家初创公司认为信用评分系统已经过时，无法反映您的信誉。这就是为什么它不使用它来计算限制。您的信用评分仍然会影响您的可变APR（从12.9％到19.9％），仅此而已。
 信用卡也有很多软件功能。例如，您可以从X1应用程序跟踪订阅，还可以为需要信用卡的免费试用生成自动到期的虚拟卡。您还会收到退款通知。
 至于奖励，您在所有购买中获得2倍积分。如果您是重度用户，并且每年在卡上花费超过$ 15,000，则可以升级到新级别并获得3倍积分。当您推荐朋友时，获得的奖励水平也会提高，这也具有病毒性元素-您每个月获得4倍积分。然后，您可以将积分用于零售合作伙伴。
 该公司承诺提供许多功能，现在银行帐户中有足够的现金可以交付。让我们看看第一个客户拿到卡后公司是否能够如实进行宣传。但是很明显，信用评分系统已经过时了。
note_en:
Spark Capital is leading the round with Jared Leto, Aaron Levie, Jeremy Stoppelman, Max Levchin and Ali Rowghani also participating. American Express veteran Ash Gupta is also joining the company as an advisor — he was the chief risk officer of American Express.
 The company says that it has attracted nearly 300,000 signups on its waitlist. I covered X1 Card  back in September and it attracted a lot of readers, so that number doesn’t surprise me.
  The X1 Card is a stainless steel Visa credit card with a different origin story. When you apply for a card, instead of determining your limits based on your credit score, the company wants to see your current and future income.
 The startup believes the credit score system is outdated and doesn’t reflect your creditworthiness. That’s why it doesn’t use it to calculate limits. Your credit score still affects your variable APR (from 12.9% to 19.9%), but that’s it.
 There are also a lot of software features that work with the credit card. For instance, you can track your subscriptions from the X1 app, you can also generate an auto-expiring virtual card for free trials that require a credit card. You also get notifications for refunds.
 As for rewards, you get 2X points on all purchases. If you’re a heavy user and you spend more than $15,000 on your card per year, you’re upgraded to a new tier and earn 3X points. There’s also a viral element as you get a boosted reward level when you refer a friend — you get 4X points for a month. You can then spend your points with retail partners.
 The company has promised a lot of features and now has enough cash in its bank account to deliver. Let’s see if the company can live up to the hype once the first customers get their cards. But it’s clear that the credit score system is outdated.
title: X1卡提供了基于当前和未来收入的限制而不是信用评分的信用卡，由Spark Capital领投了1200万美元的融资
title_en: X1 Card, which offers a credit card with limits based on current and future income instead of credit score, raises $12M funding round led by Spark Capital

note_en:
Photo: Zapier founders from left to right: Mike Knopp, Bryan Helmig and Wade Foster. Photo courtesy of Zapier
    Zapier isn’t a household name outside Silicon Valley, but the world’s top venture capitalists have long been trying to own a piece of the booming enterprise software firm. The problem for investors was that Zapier, whose software automates personal computing tasks, didn’t need their money.  It raised just $1.3 million in seed equity funding early last decade and has operated profitably for much of the last six years.
 Recently, however, storied venture firm Sequoia Capital found a way into Zapier, by buying shares from Zapier founders in the secondary market, where investors purchase private stock from existing shareholders, according to a person with knowledge of the deal. The sale was done at a valuation that appears to have surpassed $4 billion, which would be at least 40 times its annualized revenue, according to new information released by one of its earlier investors.
    Snap has promoted executive Ben Schwerin to be its new senior vice president of content and partnerships, a move that signals the company’s ambitions to compete more aggressively with TikTok and to cut more deals with media companies and app developers. As part of the change, Chief Strategy Officer Jared Grusd, who previously oversaw content and was Schwerin’s boss, will become a...

英特尔正在使用其第11代处理器更新其微型计算机的下一代计算单元（NUC）系列，并且它再次包括一个以游戏为中心的模型。 NUC 11发烧友是从2018年开始的NUC 8 Hades Canyon的后续产品，该产品设法将三A游戏性能（更不用说与全尺寸台式机相媲美的I / O）集成到一个小巧的机箱中。
 更新中包括对公司更传统的小立方体NUC Performance Mini计算机的更新，该计算机以前具有第10代处理器。升级将Wi-Fi 6和Intel Xe图形添加到i5和i7型号。英特尔还增加了一条NUC Pro系列，其中一些具有启用vPro的CPU，并且所有这些都可以驱动8K显示器。还有一个NUC 11计算单元，这只是一块板，可以集成到将来的计算机中。这不是英特尔第一次有了这个主意，但是我们很快就会对此有所了解。
   但是，NUC因其尺寸而著名，尽管Performance和Pro系列的确很小，但它们是对我们以前已有产品的较小更新。更有趣的是游戏版本。
  NUC 11发烧友（代号为“幻影峡谷”）承诺提供28W四核i7-1165G7（与2020年的Dell XPS 13相同）和RTX 2060的游戏性能，后者在英特尔宣布推出该产品的前一天就已经过时了。电脑。它还保留了使上一代产品变得出色的堆叠式I / O，包括两个Thunderbolt 4端口，2.5Gb以太网，多达六个USB 3.2 Type-A端口和Wi-Fi6。缺少HDMI 2.1（仅HDMI 2.1）。由于包含了Mini DisplayPort 1.4输出，因此在某种程度上可以原谅2.0b）。再说一次，所有这些都适合放在大约一本厚重的书本大小的外壳中。
   与其他NUC型号一样，发烧友也具有Xe集成显卡，这对于喜欢英特尔Quick Sync视频编码技术的流媒体或创意专业人士来说应该是不错的选择。尽管对于几乎最新的图形卡感到遗憾，但该计算机仍应以绝对小的包装提供出色的游戏性能，我很高兴看到英特尔仍在使用小型游戏PC。
 英特尔致力于将NUC变成具有全尺寸性能的微型游戏PC的悠久历史。它甚至与AMD合作，将专用的Vega图形放入NUC 8中，它显然希望吸引游戏玩家而不是上班族，并在顶部印有发光的骷髅标志。然后，在2020年国际消费电子展上，它展示了NUC 9 Extreme，它的目标是成为具有易于升级的计算单元的游戏PC。除了英特尔，到目前为止，尚未发布任何计算单元更新，因此，目前，它只是一台昂贵的无理由模块化游戏PC。
 英特尔尚未公布价格和上市时间，但SimplyNUC.com的“幻影峡谷”起价为1,349美元。这看起来比NUC 9 Extreme更好，在同一网站上的价格为1,599美元，并且还需要单独的微型显卡。它也击败了它更直接的前任NUC 8 Hades Canyon，对于已经使用了近两年的硬件来说，它仍然是1234美元。 
英特尔似乎采用了与过去NUC计算机相同的策略，人们可以购买一台完整的计算机或成套工具，要求他们提供RAM，存储驱动器和操作系统。 SimplyNUC的$ 1,349型号具有驱动器和RAM，但是您将不得不支付额外的费用或携带自己的Windows副本。
note_en:
Intel is updating its Next Unit of Computing (NUC) lineup of miniature computers with its 11th Gen processors, and it’s again including a gaming-focused model. The NUC 11 Enthusiast is a follow-up to  the NUC 8 Hades Canyon from 2018, which managed to pack triple-A gaming performance (not to mention I/O that rivaled a full-size desktop) into a tiny enclosure.
 Included in the refresh are updates to the company’s more traditional small-cube NUC Performance Mini computers, which previously had 10th Gen processors. The upgrade brings Wi-Fi 6 and Intel Xe graphics to the i5 and i7 models. Intel has also added a NUC Pro line, some of which have vPro-enabled CPUs and all of which can drive 8K displays. There’s also a NUC 11 compute unit, which is just a board meant to be integrated into future computers. This  isn’t the first time Intel has had that idea, but we’ll get more into that in just a moment.
   NUCs, however, are notable because of their size, and while the Performance and Pro lines are indeed very small, they’re more minor updates to what we already had before. What’s more interesting is the gaming version.
  The  NUC 11 Enthusiast, codenamed “Phantom Canyon,” promises gaming performance with a 28W quad-core i7-1165G7 (the same found in  the 2020 Dell XPS 13) and an RTX 2060,  which was made outdated the day before Intel’s announcement of the computer. It also keeps the stacked I/O that made the last generation great, including two Thunderbolt 4 ports, 2.5Gb Ethernet, a whopping six USB 3.2 Type-A ports, and Wi-Fi 6. The lack of HDMI 2.1 (it only has 2.0b) can be somewhat forgiven due to the inclusion of a Mini DisplayPort 1.4 output. And again, all of this is fitting inside an enclosure that’s roughly the size of a hefty book.
   Like the other NUC models, the Enthusiast also has Xe integrated graphics, which should be good for streamers or creative professionals that enjoy Intel’s Quick Sync video encoding technology. While it’s a shame about the almost-up-to-date graphics card, the computer should still provide a good amount of gaming performance in an absolutely diminutive package, and I’m glad to see Intel’s still working on mini gaming PCs.
 Intel has a long history of trying to make the NUC into a miniature gaming PC with full-sized performance. It even teamed up with AMD to put dedicated Vega graphics into the NUC 8, which was clearly hoping to appeal to gamers more than office workers, with a glowing skull logo emblazoned on the top. Then, at CES 2020, it showed off the NUC 9 Extreme, which aimed to be a gaming PC with an easy-to-upgrade compute unit. Except Intel, so far,  hasn’t released any updates for the compute units, so at the moment, it’s just an expensive and modular-for-no-reason gaming PC.
 Intel hasn’t released pricing and availability yet,  but SimplyNUC.com has the Phantom Canyon starting at $1,349. That looks to be a better deal than the NUC 9 Extreme, which is $1,599 on the same site and also requires a separate mini graphics card. It also beats its more direct predecessor, the NUC 8 Hades Canyon, which is still $1,234 for nearly two-year-old hardware.
 Intel appears to be using the same strategy it’s used for the NUC computers in the past, where people can buy either a complete computer or a kit where they’ll be required to provide the RAM, storage drive, and OS. SimplyNUC’s $1,349 model has the drive and RAM, but you will have to pay extra for or bring your own copy of Windows.
title: 英特尔使用第11代处理器更新了其NUC台式机产品线，增加了一款名为NUC 11 Enthusiast的专注于游戏的型号，以及可以驱动8K显示屏的NUC Pro产品线
title_en: Intel updates its NUC desktop line with 11th-gen processors, adds a gaming-focused model called NUC 11 Enthusiast, and a NUC Pro line that can drive 8K displays

 ＆＃34;我们正在寻求对被告的永久禁令，并要求他们删除其拥有的所有Facebook数据，＆＃34;罗梅罗总结。
 ＆＃34;此案是我们正在进行的国际努力的结果，该努力旨在发现并执行对那些抓取Facebook用户数据的用户，包括那些使用浏览器扩展程序来破坏人们浏览器的用户的攻击。

 例如，在2020年3月，Facebook起诉了域名注册商Namecheap及其代理服务Whoisguard＆＃34;以注册旨在通过假装与Facebook应用程序关联的方式欺骗​​人们的域名。并且经常被用于网络钓鱼，欺诈和诈骗。

note_en:
Facebook has taken legal action against the makers of malicious Chrome extensions used for scraping user-profiles and other information from Facebook's website and from users' systems without authorization.
 The two defendants developed and distributed the malicious browser extensions through the Chrome Web Store working under the " Oink and Stuff" business name.
 "They misled users into installing the extensions with a privacy policy that claimed they did not collect any personal information," Jessica Romero, Director of Platform Enforcement and Litigation,  said.
 "Four of their extensions —  Web for Instagram plus DM,  Blue Messenger,  Emoji keyboard, and  Green Messenger — were malicious and contained hidden computer code that functioned like spyware."
 The four extensions are still available for download in Google's Chrome Web Store and they currently have more than 54,000 users.
  After being installed on the users' computers, these Chrome extensions also installed malicious code in the background which allowed the defendants to scrape user data from Facebook's site.
 The malicious Chrome add-ons were also used to surreptitiously collect data unrelated to Facebook from the users' web browsers.
 While the users were browsing the Facebook website, the extensions automatically scraped account information including the victims' name, user ID, gender, relationship status, and age group among others.
  Romero added that the defendants did not compromise Facebook's security systems during their malicious activity but, instead, they only used the extensions installed on users' devices to scrape data.
 "We are seeking a permanent injunction against defendants and demanding that they delete all Facebook data in their possession," Romero concluded.
 "This case is the result of our ongoing international efforts to detect and enforce against those who scrape Facebook users’ data, including those who use browser extensions to compromise people’s browsers."
  This action is part of a  long series of instances where Facebook took legal action against entities attempting to abuse the company's platform and services.
 For instance, in March 2020,  Facebook sued domain name registrar Namecheap and its proxy service Whoisguard "for registering domain names that aim to deceive people by pretending to be affiliated with Facebook apps" and often being used "for phishing, fraud and scams."
 In October 2019, Facebook filed another  lawsuit against domain name registrar OnlineNIC and its privacy service ID Shield for allowing the registration of lookalike domains such as facebook-mails.com and www-facebook-login.com.
 The same month,  Facebook also sued Israeli cyber-surveillance firm NSO Group and its parent company Q Cyber Technologies for creating and selling a WhatsApp zero-day exploit.
 The exploit was used in May 2019 to exploit the platform's video calling system to deploy malware as part of an attack targeting over 100 high-profile targets including journalists, human rights defenders, and government officials.
title: Facebook表示已对四个恶意Chrome扩展程序的制造商提起诉讼，这些扩展程序用于从Facebook网站上抓取用户数据和其他信息
title_en: Facebook says it has filed a suit against the makers of four malicious Chrome extensions used for scraping user data and other info from Facebook's website

在今天的公告中，三星的Galaxy S21系列手机是否支持MST进行非接触式付款，这引起了一些困惑。该技术允许早期的三星设备即使在未配置NFC的终端上也可以使用无线支付，从而为读卡器模拟磁条。不幸的是，对于使用该功能的用户来说，三星向我们证实，Galaxy S21在美国不会拥有MST，这种MST的损失也将适用于未来的手机。
  我们已与三星代表双重确认了此更改，尽管该功能已在三星网站的某些规格表中列出，但其他功能中也缺少此功能。我们被告知工作表中的数据与非美国市场相对应。我们已与公司代表确认，该功能将不会在美国版Galaxy S21系列上提供。还提供了以下语句来说明该功能的删除。
 自2021年推出设备以来，由于消费者和企业迅速采用了近场通信（NFC）技术，Samsung Pay将在整个Galaxy产品组合中集中支持NFC交易。虽然未来的设备将不再包含磁条技术（MST），但是拥有以前兼容的Galaxy设备的客户将能够继续使用Samsung Pay，包括MST。
 据三星称，这一变化是由于NFC付款的激增，这使得MST已过时。尽管现在许多终端都支持NFC非接触式支付，但是MST在无法或无法负担其硬件更新的小型企业和场所中仍然很有用，并且具有许多功能（包括我们会在Android Police上看到）。
 对于那些跟踪＆＃34; missing＆＃34;的人MST具有S21上的功能，并将其与microSD插槽和捆绑的充电器一起加入列表。
  我们还应该指出，拥有Galaxy S21的任何人都可以使用Samsung Pay，这是一个更少的理由，因为其他非接触式支付服务也可以使用NFC，尤其是因为Samsung Pay拥有广告并且失去了许多奖励计划的好处。这可能足以吸引客户使用Google Pay，在过去的几个月中，Google Pay具备了一些非常出色的功能，例如能够从Google Photos和Gmail中提取收据，格子银行集成以及即将推出的Google Plex集成银行服务。相比之下，Samsung Pay的功能集确实有点费舍尔价格。
note_en:
There was some confusion at  today's announcement whether or not Samsung's Galaxy S21 series of phones support MST for contactless payments.  The technology allowed earlier Samsung devices to use wireless payments even at terminals that weren't configured with NFC, emulating a magnetic card stripe for the reader. Unfortunately for fans of the feature, Samsung confirms to us that the Galaxy S21 won't have MST in the US, and this loss of MST will apply to future phones as well.
  We doubly confirmed this change with a Samsung representative, and though the feature is listed on  some spec sheets at Samsung's site, it's also missing from others. We're told the sheets that have it correspond to non-US markets. Reaching out, we have confirmed with a company representative the feature will not be present on the US versions of the Galaxy S21 series. The following statement was also provided to explain the feature's removal.
 Due to the rapid adoption of near field communication (NFC) technology by consumers and businesses, beginning with devices launched in 2021, Samsung Pay will focus its support on NFC transactions, across the Galaxy portfolio. While future devices will no longer include magnetic stripe technology (MST), customers with previous, compatible Galaxy devices will be able to continue using Samsung Pay, including MST.
 According to Samsung, the change is due to the proliferation of NFC payments, which make MST obsolete. While it's true that many terminals now support NFC contactless payments, MST was still useful at smaller businesses and venues which failed to or couldn't afford to update their hardware, and it's a feature many (including us here at Android Police) will miss.
 For folks keeping track of "missing" features on the S21, MST joins the list together with the  microSD slot and  bundled charger.
  We should also point out, it's one less reason for anyone with a Galaxy S21 to use Samsung Pay, as other contactless payments services can also use NFC, and especially since Samsung Pay  has ads and  lost many of its rewards program benefits. That could be enough to tempt customers over to  Google Pay, which has picked up some pretty great features over the last few months, like the ability to  pull receipts from Google Photos and Gmail, Plaid bank integration, and an upcoming  Google Plex integrated banking service. Samsung Pay's feature set does feel a bit Fisher-Price in comparison.
title: 三星确认Galaxy S21系列和未来的设备将不支持三星Pay的MST，这使其可以与旧式磁条终端一起使用
title_en: Samsung confirms Galaxy S21 series and future devices will not support MST for Samsung Pay, which enabled it to be used with legacy magnetic strip terminals

note: 不出所料，三星的新Galaxy S21手机将没有包装盒中的电源适配器。 每部手机随附USB-C至USB-C电缆，但您需要提供自己的充电器。 三星正将其25W电源适配器的价格从35美元下调至20美元，以弥补这一事实。 还需要记住的是，该公司的旗舰产品系列今年起价为800美元，而去年购买Galaxy S20则需要支付1,000美元。  显然，此举是苹果公司采取的类似行动。 去年，该公司停止在其新的Apple Watch和iPhone 12设备中包括电源适配器。 与苹果公司一样，三星也提到了对环境的担忧，这是其在包装盒中不包括电源适配器的原因。
note_en:
As expected,  Samsung’s new  Galaxy S21 phones won’t come with a power adapter in the box. Each phone ships with a USB-C to USB-C cable, but you’ll need to provide your own charger. Samsung is reducing the price of its 25W power adapter to $20, down from $35, to make up for the fact. It’s also worth keeping in mind the company’s flagship lineup starts at $800 this year, as opposed to the $1,000 you had to pay to buy the Galaxy S20 last year.
 The move obviously follows a similar one from Apple. Last year, the company stopped including a power adapter with its new  Apple Watch and iPhone 12 devices. And like Apple, Samsung cited a concern for the environment as its reason for not including a power adapter in the box.
title: 三星的整个Galaxy S21手机产品系列都不会在包装盒中包括电源适配器。 三星将其25W适配器的价格从35美元下调至20美元

Galaxy S21 Ultra：幻影银色或幻影黑色。 256GB和512GB型号只会采用幻影黑色。
                                              每部手机的费用将根据存储量而有所不同。以下是我们目前每种型号的美国价格。
           三星正在其网站上进行预订，提供运营商版本以及未锁定的版本。如果您从三星订购，则订购Galaxy S21可获得$ 100的信用额，订购S21 Plus可获得$ 150的信用额，或购买S21 Ultra可获得$ 200的信用额。该信用额可用于通过三星为您的手机购买其他物品或配件。预购还将免费获得三星新的SmartTag蓝牙跟踪器之一。
 在限定时间内，三星将通过合格的以旧换新，为任何S21型号减价700美元。
 这家零售巨头正在接受所有三种S21型号的预订，但是，您需要成为Verizon或AT＆amp; T的客户（或愿意切换到任一运营商）。
 您可以在Best Buy网站上找到几乎所有的S21系列配置，现在可以预订。唯一标记为即将上市（因此您无法订购）的型号是所有三种S21型号的解锁版本。
  Verizon正在为所有三种型号进行预订，并提供免费的Galaxy S21 Plus，该产品具有合格的以旧换新，并且当您注册选择＆＃34; select＆＃34;时，无限计划。 
您可以通过AT＆amp; T预订任何新型号。该运营商提供的优惠从新版Galaxy Buds Pro的50％折扣到包含以旧换新和分期付款计划的免费Galaxy S21折扣。

note_en:
This story is part of  CES, where our editors will bring you the latest news and the hottest gadgets of the entirely virtual CES 2021.    Samsung on Thursday announced a trio of  smartphones -- the   Galaxy S21, Galaxy S21 Plus and S21 Ultra. All three phones have new designs, with a new, metal-framed camera array on the back and improved camera features. If you opt for the high-end Ultra, you can use an   S Pen stylus to write or draw on the screen, just as you can on one of Samsung's Note models (but the stylus won't come with it).
 This year, all three Galaxy S21 models offer full  5G, and now you don't have to pick between a smoother scrolling experience or the display's high resolution -- you can now have both at the same time.
     Preorders are open right now, and all three Galaxy S21 models are expected to start showing up in a couple of weeks, on Friday, Jan. 29. Everything you need to know about getting Samsung's latest and greatest can be found below.
   Preorders for all three models are open right now. Orders will begin to arrive on Jan. 29.
  Samsung is giving those who preorder any of the three Galaxy S21 models a free   Samsung Galaxy SmartTag Bluetooth tracker, along with a credit that can be used in the Samsung store. The credit is either $100, $150 or $200, depending on whether you buy the S21, S21 Plus or S21 Ultra, respectively.
                                               Galaxy S21: Phantom violet, phantom gray, phantom pink and phantom white. The 256GB version is only available in phantom gray.
 Galaxy S21 Plus: Phantom violet, phantom silver and phantom black. The 256GB version is only available in phantom black.
 Galaxy S21 Ultra: Phantom silver or phantom black. The 256GB and 512GB models will only come in phantom black.
                                              The cost for each phone will vary based on storage amount. Below are the US prices we currently know for each model.
           Samsung is taking preorders on its website, offering carrier versions along with an unlocked variant. If you order from Samsung, you'll get a $100 credit for ordering the Galaxy S21, $150 for ordering the S21 Plus or $200 for an S21 Ultra. The credit can be used to buy another item or accessories for your phone through Samsung. Preorders will also receive one of Samsung's new SmartTag Bluetooth trackers for free.
 For a limited time, Samsung is giving up to $700 off the cost of any S21 model with an eligible trade-in.
 The retail giant is taking preorders of all three S21 models, however, you'll need to be a Verizon or AT&T customer (or be willing to switch to either carrier).
 You can find almost every configuration of the S21 lineup on Best Buy's site, available to preorder right now. The only model that's marked as coming soon, so you can't order it, is the unlocked version of all three S21 models.
  Verizon is taking preorders for all three models, and is offering a free Galaxy S21 Plus with a qualified trade-in and when you sign up for "select" unlimited plans.
 You can preorder any of the new models through AT&T. The carrier is offering deals that range from 50% off the new Galaxy Buds Pro to a free Galaxy S21 with a trade-in and installment plan.
 Preorder any of the three Galaxy S21 models from T-Mobile. You can get a Galaxy S21 for free with a qualified trade-in. You aren't required to add a line for this deal. There's also a buy-one-get-one promotion.
 Preorders for Spectrum customers are now open. If you switch to the carrier, you'll get $100 with an eligible trade-in.
 Visible isn't doing preorders, but will instead start selling the S21 and S21 Plus on launch day, Jan. 29. The prepaid carrier is taking a little bit off the price of both models, with the S21 starting at $792 and the S21 Plus starting at $984.
 Starting Friday, Jan. 15, Altice Mobile will start taking preorders for the Galaxy S21, S21 Plus and S21 Ultra. Customers can get up to $200 in Samsung credit for preordering.

三星的最新旗舰产品就在这里：Galaxy S21，Galaxy S21 Plus和Galaxy S21 Ultra。前两个产品的价格分别为799美元和999美元，这是三星多年来制造的最便宜的旗舰手机，而1,199美元的S21 Ultra的目标不仅是三星产品阵容中最顶级的手机，而且最好的手机，期间。
 上周，我能够简短地试用这三款设备，并且它们都共享一种新的设计语言，这使我对三星智能手机的期望值大大提高。与去年的S20系列相比，内部变化更具增量性，在某些情况下，这是有意退后的一步。所有三款手机均可预订，并将于1月29日推出。
 新设备可以有效地分为两类：旨在实现更加钱包友好型旗舰体验的S21和S21 Plus，以及无懈可击的终极三星智能手机S21 Ultra，旨在提供最优质的服务。可以拨打电话-并提供价格标签。
   与过去几年不同，这次S21和S21 Plus几乎是相同的设备，除非屏幕尺寸，电池尺寸以及一个或两个其他次要细节存在明显差异。
 但是，这两款手机最重要的变化实际上并不是硬件本身。经过多年的价格攀升之后，三星终于扭转了局面，今年S21和S21 Plus的起价分别为799美元和999美元。这比去年的型号便宜了200美元。
 为了做到这一点，三星做出了一些妥协，以达到较低的价格。显示器的尺寸与去年的型号相同，分别为6.2英寸（S21）和6.7英寸（S21 Plus），但掉落了微妙的弧形边缘，多年来一直是三星的标志。这些平板屏幕的分辨率也从去年的3200 x 1440面板降低到了更适中的2400 x 1080分辨率。两款手机虽然现在都可以动态调整，但仍提供120Hz的刷新率。最后，RAM也已从12GB降至8GB。
   较小的Galaxy S21需要更多的取舍：背板是由聚碳酸酯塑料而不是玻璃制成的，并且缺少超宽带收音机（S21 Plus和Ultra提供了与三星新的Galaxy SmartTag更好集成的功能）。 
最后，三星遵循苹果的脚步，不再在盒子中装充电砖或耳机，并出于类似的环境原因对其进行辩解（尽管三星也可能通过不捆绑这些部件而节省了几美元）。
 最大的问题是：那些公认的小变化是否值得降价200美元？我们将不得不等到新的S21和S21 Plus能够适应他们的步伐，然后再进行我们的审查。
   不过，并非所有产品都降级了：该处理器是高通公司最新的Snapdragon 888芯片组，有望在整体性能上做出重大改进。这两款设备均实现了全面的5G集成，并支持mmWave和6GHz以下网络。指纹传感器已升级为高通公司的新型3D Sonic Sensor Gen 2，它应该更快，更容易使用。而且电池仍然很大：S21仍提供强劲的4,000mAh电池，而S21 Plus已升级为更大的4,800mAh电池。
 但是，今年最大的更新是新设计，该设计可以使三星多年来使用的玻璃夹层三明治保持更新外观。这很容易成为我对新手机的最爱。经过多年的处理，凸出的相机凸起已经成为在我们的智能手机上提供更大的传感器和更好的镜头的事实成本，S21和S21 Plus则采用了相机凸起而不是尝试解决它。
  S21和S21 Plus仍然具有摄像头凸块，但看起来像无缝金属片，可以从手机的匹配侧自然流出，它们看起来不错，边缘光滑且具有柔和的两色调设计，看起来确实不错。
 不过，摄像头系统本身与去年相比基本没有变化。 S21和S21 Plus均提供12百万像素的广角相机，12百万像素的超广角相机和64百万像素的远摄镜头，以及10百万像素的自拍相机。 （S21 Plus不再具有深度传感器。）如果听起来相似，那是因为硬件实际上与去年的Galaxy S20和S20 Plus手机上的摄像头相同。三星在软件方面进行了一些改进，包括30倍“太空变焦”模式，新的“导演视角”视频模式，使得在拍摄时可以在镜头之间轻松切换，以及三星的“单摄”模式的新增功能。
 三星告诉我们，默认情况下，它不会再使脸部过于光滑，而是使其成为一种选择。现在是时候了。 
如果说S21和S21 Plus在智能手机设计上步履蹒跚，那么Galaxy S21 Ultra就是一款手机，它可以想象如果您不必做出任何让步的话会是什么样。这款手机承诺会在三星的硬件和Android产品中提供最好的功能-如果您愿意为此付费的话。
 S21 Ultra起价为1,199美元（不包括S Pen手写笔或表壳的任何额外价格），是三星第三款最高级的手机，是去年S20 Ultra的继任者。它与原始照片有很多共同之处，包括大屏幕，108兆像素的摄像头和顶级规格。
 但是S21 Ultra是去年型号的改进版本，它看起来可以平滑一些锐利边缘以获得更好的体验-实际上，在某些情况下，这要归功于三星新的圆形设计语言（类似于S21和S21 Plus）。
  查看规格表，S21 Ultra几乎检查所有框：一个6.8英寸3200 x 1440 OLED显示屏，刷新率最高为120Hz（与去年不同，S21 Ultra可以达到全分辨率的刷新率）。配备12GB RAM，5,000mAh电池，最大512GB存储空间以及高通公司最新的Snapdragon 888处理器。很难想象三星会在这里塞满更多东西。
 当然，仅仅因为三星无法将其装入手机中并不意味着它已经完成了添加功能。 S20 Ultra将是首款支持三星S Pen手写笔的Galaxy S手机，后者可以完成S Pen功能。如果您最近使用过Galaxy Note，那么您将会对预期有所了解。唯一的问题：$ 1,199美元以上的手机包装盒中没有包含手写笔：它将额外收取40美元，或捆绑在一起的70美元，其中包括可以同时存放手机和手写笔的盒子。这些情况非常笨重，为已经很大的手机增加了额外的宽度。
   然后是摄像机。 S20 Ultra的相机试图通过其108兆像素的传感器和100倍变焦模式拍摄月球，从而保证摄影效果可以与手机其余部分的“超凡”形象媲美。
 该技术非常酷，但是存在很多问题。 S21 Ultra有望第二次提供更精致的体验：相机具有新的焦距，可以拍摄稍宽的照片；新的激光自动对焦系统（类似于Note 20 Ultra）可以帮助解决最后的对焦问题。年的模型，支持12位色深，以及更精细的去马赛克处理。三星还致力于为用户提供更多控制权，例如基于AI的照片增强功能，例如允许您调整平滑度。是否足以解决原始文件的问题，还需要进一步测试。 
不过，S21 Ultra不仅仅是那种大的108兆像素传感器。它还有另外三个后置摄像头。当然，有一台12百万像素的超宽相机。然后没有一台，而是两台单独的10百万像素远摄相机：一台具有3倍变焦，一台具有10倍变焦，三星表示，这种摄影机旨在为用户提供更灵活的拍摄方法。所有这些附加传感器的缺点是，它将摄像机阵列的宽度扩大到几乎是常规S21和S21 Ultra型号的两倍，这会稍微破坏外观。
  与三星过去提供的产品相比，Galaxy S21，S21 Plus和S21 Ultra是更精心设计的产品系列。这是一种平衡的举动：一方面，这些设备表明三星已经开始内部化一种思想，即并非每部手机都需要其规格表的所有方面都提高到绝对最大值。另外，它还提供了Galaxy S21 Ultra，这是一款真正为真正想要这种设备的高级用户而设计的手机。
 去年，我们才开始看到这种哲学，那就是售价699美元的Galaxy S20 FE，这是一款特意便宜的三星手机，旨在以更实惠的价格提供优质的体验。售价799美元和999美元的S21和S21 Plus感觉像是对这一理念的延伸，从利基的“风扇版”设备扩展到了三星硬件中最主流的设备。三星几乎无所不在的销售因素之一，S21可能是最近记忆中最实惠的Galaxy S设备。
 多年来，三星一直致力于在纸张上制造出最好的手机。 S21系列感觉就像是尝试针对不同客户的不同需求制造出最好的手机，而不是简单地召集最昂贵的手机。在经济不景气的情况下，这可能是正确的策略，但这意味着其较小的手机并没有那么令人印象深刻。使用S21，如果您想要三星提供的最好的东西，那么您将不得不购买最大的东西。
 更正：Galaxy S21 Ultra起价为1,199美元。本文在一个段落中错误地将价格列出为$ 1,119。此外，S21 Ultra和S21 Plus具有玻璃后盖，而不是铝。
note_en:
Samsung’s latest flagships are here: the Galaxy S21, Galaxy S21 Plus, and the Galaxy S21 Ultra. The first two — which start at $799 and $999, respectively — mark some of the least expensive flagship phones that Samsung has made in years, while the $1,199 S21 Ultra aims to be not just the top-specced phone in Samsung’s lineup, but also the best phone, period.
 I was able to briefly try out all three devices last week, and they all share a new design language that throws out a lot of what I’d come to expect from a Samsung smartphone. The internal changes are more incremental from last year’s S20 lineup, though, and in some cases, they’re an intentional step back.  All three phones are available to preorder and will launch on January 29th.
 The new devices can effectively be divided into two categories: the S21 and S21 Plus, which are aiming to achieve a more wallet-friendly flagship experience, and the S21 Ultra, the ultimate Samsung smartphone that cuts no corners and aims to offer the most premium phone possible — with a price tag to match.
   Unlike past years, the S21 and S21 Plus are almost identical devices this time around, barring the obvious differences in screen size, battery size, and one or two other minor details.
 But the most important change with these two phones isn’t actually the hardware itself; it’s after years of creeping prices, Samsung has finally reversed course, with the S21 and S21 Plus actually starting at $799 and $999, respectively, this year. That’s a full $200 less than last year’s models.
 In order to accomplish that, Samsung made a few compromises in order to reach those lower price points. The displays, while the same 6.2-inch (S21) and 6.7-inch (S21 Plus) sizes as last year’s models, have dropped the subtly curved edges that have been a Samsung hallmark for years. Those flat panel screens also feature a step down in resolution from the 3200 x 1440 panels on last year’s models to a more modest 2400 x 1080 resolution. Both phones still offer 120Hz refresh rates, although it’s dynamically adjusted now. Lastly, the RAM has also been dropped from 12GB to 8GB.
   The smaller Galaxy S21 makes additional trade-offs: the back panel is made of polycarbonate plastic, not glass, and it lacks an ultra-wideband radio (which the S21 Plus and Ultra offer for better integration with Samsung’s new Galaxy SmartTag.)
 Lastly, Samsung is following in Apple’s footsteps by no longer including a charging brick or headphones in the box, justifying it with similar environmental reasons (although Samsung will also likely save a few dollars by not bundling those parts).
 The big question is: are those admittedly small changes worth the $200 price cuts? We’ll have to wait until we can put the new S21 and S21 Plus through their paces in our upcoming review.
   It’s not all downgrades, however: the processor is Qualcomm’s latest Snapdragon 888 chipset, which promises big improvements in overall performance. There’s comprehensive 5G integration on both devices, with support for mmWave and sub-6GHz networks. The fingerprint sensor has been upgraded to Qualcomm’s new 3D Sonic Sensor Gen 2, which should be faster and easier to use. And the batteries are still big: the S21 still offers a beefy 4,000mAh battery, and the S21 Plus has been upgraded to an even larger 4,800mAh one.
 But the biggest update this year is the new design, which ditches the glass-slab sandwich that Samsung’s been using for years for an updated look. And it’s easily my favorite thing about the new phones. After years of dealing with the protruding camera bumps that have been the de facto cost of offering bigger sensors and better lenses on our smartphones, the S21 and S21 Plus embrace the camera bump instead of trying to work around it.
  The S21 and S21 Plus still have camera bumps, but they look good as seamless pieces of metal that flow out naturally from the matching sides of the phones, with smooth edges and spiffy two-tone designs that look really nice.
 Largely unchanged from last year, though, is the camera system itself. Both the S21 and S21 Plus offer a 12-megapixel wide-angle camera, a 12-megapixel ultrawide camera, and a 64-megapixel telephoto lens, along with a 10-megapixel selfie camera. (The S21 Plus no longer has a depth sensor.) If those sound similar, it’s because the hardware is virtually identical to the cameras found on last year’s Galaxy S20 and S20 Plus phones. Samsung has made some improvements on the software side of things, including the 30x “Space Zoom” mode, a new “Director’s View” mode for video that makes it easy to swap between lenses while filming, and additions to Samsung’s “Single Take” mode.
 Samsung has told us that it will no longer over-smooth faces by default, instead making it an option. It’s high time that happened.
    If the S21 and S21 Plus are more restrained steps back in smartphone design, the Galaxy S21 Ultra is a phone that imagines what it would look like if you never had to make any compromises. It’s a phone that promises the very best of what Samsung’s hardware and Android can offer — if you’re willing to pay for it.
 Starting at $1,199 (a price that doesn’t include any extras like an S Pen stylus or a case), the S21 Ultra is Samsung’s third superlatively named phone, the successor to last year’s S20 Ultra. And it has a lot in common with the original, including a massive screen, a 108-megapixel camera, and top-of-the-line specs.
 But the S21 Ultra is a more refined version of last year’s model, one that looks to smooth out some of the sharp edges for a better experience — literally, in some cases, thanks to Samsung’s new rounded design language (which is similar to the S21 and S21 Plus).
  Looking at the spec sheet, the S21 Ultra checks almost every box: a 6.8-inch 3200 x 1440 OLED display, with refresh rates up to 120Hz (a refresh rate the S21 Ultra can hit at full resolution, unlike last year). There’s 12GB of RAM, a 5,000mAh battery, up to 512GB of storage, and Qualcomm’s latest Snapdragon 888 processor. It’s hard to imagine Samsung cramming more in here.
 Of course, just because Samsung can’t fit it into the phone doesn’t mean it’s done adding features. The S20 Ultra will be the first Galaxy S phone to support Samsung’s S Pen stylus, which does S Pen things. If you’ve used a Galaxy Note recently, you’ll have a good idea of what to expect. The only quibble: the stylus isn’t included in the box with your $1,199-plus phone: it’ll cost an extra $40, or $70 in a bundle that includes a case that can store both the phone and the stylus. And those cases are pretty bulky, adding extra width to an already pretty big phone.
   And then there are the cameras. The S20 Ultra’s camera tried to shoot for the moon, with its 108-megapixel sensor and 100x zoom mode that promised photography that could live up to the “ultra” moniker of the rest of the phone.
 The technology was incredibly cool, but it had plenty of issues. The S21 Ultra promises to offer a more refined experience for the second time around: the camera has a new focal length for slightly wider shots, a new laser autofocus system (similar to the Note 20 Ultra) that should help with the focusing issues on last year’s model, support for 12-bit color depth, and a more refined demosaicing process. Samsung is also working to give users more control over things like its AI-based photo enhancements, allowing you to adjust the level of smoothing, for example. Whether that’s all enough to solve the issues of the original is something that will require further testing.
 The S21 Ultra isn’t just about that big 108-megapixel sensor, though. It also has an additional three rear cameras. There is a 12-megapixel ultrawide camera, of course. Then there is not one but  two separate 10-megapixel telephoto cameras: one with a 3X zoom and one with 10X zoom, which Samsung says is designed to give users more flexibility in how they approach shots. The downside of all those added sensors is that it expands the camera array to almost twice as wide as the regular S21 and S21 Ultra models, which spoils the look a little bit.
  The Galaxy S21, S21 Plus, and S21 Ultra are a more deliberate lineup than Samsung’s past offerings. It’s a balancing act: with one hand, the devices show that Samsung has started to internalize the idea that not every phone needs every aspect of its spec sheet ramped up to the absolute max; with the other, it offers the Galaxy S21 Ultra, a phone that does just that for the power users who truly want that kind of device.
 It’s a philosophy that we started to see last year with the $699 Galaxy S20 FE, an intentionally cheaper Samsung phone that aimed to offer a premium experience at a more affordable price. The $799 and $999 S21 and S21 Plus feel like extensions of that philosophy, expanded from a niche “fan edition” device to the most mainstream of Samsung’s hardware. Factor in the almost omnipresent Samsung sales, and the S21 could be the most affordable Galaxy S device in recent memory.
 For years, Samsung has focused on making the best phones on paper that it could. The S21 lineup feels like an attempt to make the best phone for the different needs of different customers, rather than simply the most excessive phone it can muster. It might be the right strategy in the midst of a struggling economy, but it means that its smaller phones aren’t quite as impressive. With the S21, if you want the best thing Samsung has to offer, you are going to have to buy the biggest thing.
 Correction:  The Galaxy S21 Ultra starts at $1,199. This article incorrectly listed the price in one paragraph as $1,119. Additionally, the S21 Ultra and S21 Plus feature glass backs, not aluminum.

可靠的苹果分析师郭明池（Ming-Chi Kuo）今天发布了有关即将推出的MacBook Pro的一些有趣的细节。 Kuo将其描述为五年来笔记本电脑的首次重大重新设计，并且即使在最新的2019年16英寸MacBook Pro上也将进行重大升级。新的MacBook Pro将具有14英寸和16英寸的尺寸，并具有ARM Apple硅片而不是Intel CPU。
 但是，Kuo分享了一些令人惊讶的花絮，即Touch Bar将不再支持物理功能键，MagSafe充电连接器与更多内置的IO端口一起返回，这意味着大多数用户将不需要购买额外的加密狗。到郭…
  据说新的机箱设计在机器的上半部和下半部均具有方形的侧面，从2018年iPad Pro开始并在2020 iPhone 12系列中采用了扁平边缘设计趋势。 （当然，新的MacBook Pro系列将配备Apple ARM CPU，但Kuo在今天的报告中并未分享有关该芯片性能方面的细节。）
 显然，Touch Bar已被完全删除，而新款MacBook Pro仅提供了一排物理功能键。鉴于去年秋天发布了低端13英寸M1 MacBook Pro，并且确实配备了Touch Bar，尽管所有第一批M1 Mac都具有与上一代产品相同的硬件设计，这一点很有趣。
 当2016年MacBook Pro面世时，苹果公司宣布Touch Bar作为Mac输入法的未来，但该功能从未真正流行起来或发挥其潜力。许多Mac用户只是想用手指就能感觉到的物理按键，而不必低头看着触摸屏。自推出以来已有五年，看来苹果公司正在听取这些反馈意见，Touch Bar将从MacBook系列中逐步淘汰。
 Kuo并未确切说明将提供哪些IO端口。当前的高端13英寸和16英寸MacBook Pro型号具有四个Thunderbolt 3 USB-C端口，每侧两个。就上下文而言，2016年之前的型号提供了SD卡插槽，HDMI和USB-A端口。 USB-C端口在技术上是优越的，并且可以在更高的带宽上传输数据，但是当前的MacBook Pro仅具有USB-C的事实通常意味着用户必须购买并携带一系列的加密狗。 SD卡插槽之类的东西内置起来要优雅得多。
 全USB-C设计的一个主要优点是客户可以连接电源并使用机器两侧的任何端口为其MacBook Pro充电。郭说，MagSafe电源连接器正在恢复，但没有提及这是否意味着左侧或右侧的充电能力将丢失。请注意，该报告明确指定了MagSafe的退货是通过磁性电源连接器（如旧的MacBooks）进行的，而不是通过某些无线感应充电系统（如iPhone 12的“ MagSafe”）进行的。 
总而言之，听起来苹果将满足错过或可能仍在使用2015年MacBook Pro的客户的需求，并提供更多传统的IO端口，物理功能键和MagSafe供电。 这种传统功能集将包含在全新的外部设计中，当然，这些机器将由苹果设计的快速ARM芯片提供动力。 新款14英寸和16英寸MacBook Pro预计将于2021年第三季度首次亮相。  您正在阅读9to5Mac，这是专家，日复一日地发布有关Apple及其周围生态系统的新闻。 请务必查看我们的主页以获取所有最新新闻，并在Twitter，Facebook和LinkedIn上关注9to5Mac以保持循环。 不知道从哪里开始？ 查看我们的独家故事，评论，操作方法，并订阅我们的YouTube频道
note_en:
Reliable Apple analyst Ming-Chi Kuo today released some interesting details about the upcoming MacBook Pro. Kuo describes this as the first major redesign of the laptop in five years, and will represent a significant upgrade over even the latest  2019 16-inch MacBook Pro. The new MacBook Pro will feature 14-inch and 16-inch sizes and feature ARM Apple silicon instead of Intel CPUs.
 However, Kuo shares some much more surprising tidbits, namely that the Touch Bar will be gone in favor of physical function keys, the MagSafe charging connector returns along with more built-in IO ports that mean most users will not need to purchase additional dongles according to Kuo …
  The new chassis design is said to feature squared-off sides in both the top and bottom halves of the machine, joining the flat edge design trend started with the 2018 iPad Pro and adopted in the 2020 iPhone 12 series. (The new MacBook Pro series will of course feature Apple ARM CPUs, but Kuo does not share specifics about how the chip will fare performance-wise in today’s report.)
 The Touch Bar is apparently entirely removed, with the new MacBook Pro simply offering a physical row of function keys. This is interesting in light of the fact that the low-end 13-inch M1 MacBook Pro was released last fall, and did come with a Touch Bar, although all of the first M1 Macs essentially featured identical hardware designs compared to the previous generation.
 Apple heralded the Touch Bar as the future of the Mac input method when the 2016 MacBook Pro was unveiled, but the feature never really caught on or lived up to its potential. Many Mac users simply want physical keys they can feel with their fingers without looking down at a touch screen. Five years since its introduction, it seems Apple is listening to that feedback and the Touch Bar will be phased out from the MacBook lineup.
 Kuo does not specify exactly what IO ports will be offered. The current high-end 13-inch and 16-inch MacBook Pro models feature four Thunderbolt 3 USB-C ports, two on each side. For context, the pre-2016 models offered a SD card slot, HDMI and USB-A ports. USB-C ports are technically superior and can carry data at much higher bandwidth, but the fact the current MacBook Pro features USB-C exclusively typically means users have to buy and carry an array of dongles. Things like the SD card slot are much more elegant being built in.
 One major advantage of the all-USB-C design was that customers can connect to power and charge their MacBook Pro with any port on either side of the machine. Kuo says that a MagSafe power connector is returning but does not mention if this means the ability to charge on the left or right will be lost. Note that the report explicitly specifies the return of MagSafe is via a magnetic power connector like MacBooks of old, and not some wireless inductive charging system like the iPhone 12’s “MagSafe”.
 In summary, it sounds like Apple will be satisfying the demands of customers who miss — or maybe still use — the 2015 MacBook Pro with the return of more traditional IO ports, physical function keys and MagSafe for power. This traditional feature set will be encased in all-new external design, and of course the machines will be powered by blazing fast Apple-designed ARM chips. The new 14-inch and 16-inch MacBook Pro is expected to debut in the third quarter of 2021.
  You’re reading 9to5Mac — experts who break news about Apple and its surrounding ecosystem, day after day. Be sure to check out  our homepage for all the latest news, and follow 9to5Mac on  Twitter,  Facebook, and  LinkedIn to stay in the loop. Don’t know where to start?
